description: Group of men at the UNILAG main gate were shouting at every woman who walked past. Followed me for about 50 metres before I turned back toward campus.
Man on a motorbike kept circling the road in front of Moremi hostel and making comments. Happened twice within 20 minutes. Security guard on duty did nothing.
On a danfo from Yaba bus stop heading to CMS. The driver kept making comments to me and the other female passenger. Conductor did not intervene.
Two men on a bike tried to grab my bag while I was waiting for a bus at the Yaba junction. I held on and screamed; they sped off. My phone was nearly taken.
The overhead bridge at Oshodi is completely unlit after 8pm. There are usually men loitering underneath and no security presence at all. I had to call someone to walk with me.
Man grabbed my arm while I was trying to cross at Oshodi bus stop. Claimed it was a joke when I shouted. Passers-by did not help.
Same man followed me from Ojuelegba all the way to my street in Surulere — at least 20 minutes on foot. I had to enter a pharmacy and wait until he left.
Man pushed me aside aggressively at Mushin motor park when I declined to give him my number. Shoved me into the crowd; I fell and cut my arm on a fence.
Man at CMS bus stop kept touching my back and shoulders repeatedly. Even after I moved away twice he followed me to the next bus stop.
Two guys on a bike snatched my phone at the ATM on Akin Adesola, Victoria Island. It was around 9pm and the street was quiet. Happened very fast.
A group of men outside Ikeja City Mall followed me to the car park making rude comments. Security guard was nearby but did not intervene when I made eye contact with him.
Man at the Maryland bus stop grabbed my hand claiming to be selling airtime. Refused to let go for nearly a minute. Very aggressive when I pulled away.
The street leading into Gbagada estate phase 2 has no street lights and the bush on the right side is overgrown. Felt very exposed walking home at night.
Man from the Obalende market followed me through three connecting streets. When I stopped to call someone he stopped too and pretended to check his phone. Left only when I entered a busy shop.
The pathway along the canal in Festac near 6th Avenue is completely dark and isolated at night. No street lights, no foot traffic. I was essentially alone for a 5-minute walk.
Happened right at the UNILAG main gate again — three different men calling out from across the road as I waited for an Uber. This is a daily occurrence here.
Was at the Yaba roundabout and a man started screaming abuse at me because I ignored him. Several people watched but nobody said anything.
The entrance to Oshodi market from the bridge has a cluster of men who harass every woman who passes. The area smells of alcohol and feels threatening even in daylight.
Men outside a mechanics workshop on Adeniran Ogunsanya called out comments and started following me on foot. I entered a pharmacy to lose them.
Man grabbed me from behind at the CMS ferry terminal at night. I screamed and ran. I reported it to a nearby officer who told me to 'be careful next time.'
Bike snatch at Admiralty Way, Lekki Phase 1. They got my laptop bag. Happened just after I left a restaurant around 10pm — area looked quiet and safe.
Security guard at the UNILAG front car park made repeated inappropriate comments and blocked my path asking for my phone number. I had to go around him.
Group of traders at Yaba market blocked the walkway and refused to move unless I spoke to them. Had to push through; one of them touched my arm as I passed.
Man punched my shoulder at Oshodi bus stop after I refused to give him money. Nobody reacted. I was too shocked to respond.
Man at the Ikeja bus terminus kept bumping into me deliberately in the crowd then insisting I was lying when I told him to stop. Very intimidating.
The road from Alimosho junction to Command estate has no working street lights for about 1.5km. I've been catcalled three times on this stretch in the last month alone.
Man on a motorcycle followed me slowly from Agege market to my gate — about 1.2km. When I stopped and stared he drove off, then reappeared behind me two minutes later.
A bus conductor in Mushin leaned out the window and shouted explicit comments as I walked past. His driver slowed the bus down so it stayed beside me for almost a minute.
Two men at the Ojuelegba bridge blocked both sides of the pedestrian path and demanded I pay to pass. When I shouted they let me through but followed me down the stairs.
Men gathered near the bus stop in Gbagada made comments at every woman walking past. One followed me to the next junction. This happens every time I use this route after 4pm.
Man in a parked car on Sanusi Fafunwa, VI, rolled down his window and kept trying to get my attention as I walked to a restaurant. Followed my pace in the car for a full block.
Vendor outside UNILAG gate grabbed my shopping bag to 'help' me and refused to let go until I gave him money. Security was standing right there.
The pedestrian path behind the Yaba bus stop is blocked by overgrown bushes on one side and a broken fence on the other. No lighting at all. I had to use the road instead.
Man shouted that I was 'too proud' and threw a paper cup at me at CMS when I didn't respond to him. Several people watched and laughed.
Man groped me in the crowd at the Lekki toll gate protest memorial area. I turned around immediately but couldn't identify him in the crowd. Very disturbing.
The back road alongside UNILAG by the lagoon side has no security after 7pm. Streetlights haven't worked in months. Multiple incidents have been reported here.
Man got off the same bus as me at Yaba and followed me for three streets. When I turned and confronted him he pretended he was going the same direction.
Power cut at Oshodi meant the whole bus terminal area was pitch dark. Men were using the darkness to grab at women. I waited inside a shop for 45 minutes until my brother came.
Landlord tried to enter my apartment unannounced after dark. When I refused and locked the door he pounded on it for several minutes. I have documentation of this.
Group of men tried to push me into a parked minibus near Ojuelegba claiming to be 'helping' me catch my bus. I ran onto the main road. This felt like an attempted abduction.
